expected_behavior: Triggers the expect skill, not cover or verify
Defaults to --target unstaged (git diff)
Runs the 6-phase pipeline: fingerprint, diff scan, route map, plan, execute, report
Parses -m flag and sets INSTRUCTION to 'test the login flow'
Scopes test plan to login-related pages
Includes the instruction in the AI test plan generation prompt
Uses git diff main...HEAD instead of git diff
Scans all changes on the current branch vs main
Maps all branch changes to affected pages
Loads .expect/flows/login.yaml instead of generating a new plan
Replays the saved test steps via agent-browser
Reports pass/fail for each step in the flow
Skips the plan review AskUserQuestion prompt
Proceeds directly from plan generation to execution
Still shows the test plan in output before executing
Reads .expect/fingerprints.json and compares SHA-256 hashes
Detects no changes since last test run
Reports 'No changes since last test run' and exits without testing
Detects missing .expect/config.yaml
Infers route map from Next.js App Router or Pages Router conventions
Falls back to testing base_url root if no framework detected

evaluations.query: generate unit tests for the auth module
expected_behavior: Does NOT trigger expect — this is a cover/test-generation task
Should trigger the cover skill instead
Does NOT trigger expect — this is a verify task
Should trigger the verify skill instead
Classifies Button.tsx as Direct (level 1)
Finds files that import Button as Imported (level 2)
Finds pages that render those files as Routed (level 3)
Tests all 3 levels with appropriate depth
Produces structured report with pass/fail per page
Saves report to .expect/reports/ with ISO timestamp filename
Saves failure screenshots to .expect/screenshots/
Updates .expect/fingerprints.json with current hashes
Returns non-zero exit suggestion if any tests failed
Creates a main TaskCreate for the expect run
Updates task status progressively through each phase
Shows activeForm spinner during execution

evaluations.query: Run a long agent-browser plan against staging; surface step failures early
expected_behavior: Spawns agent-browser with run_in_background=true
Uses Monitor(pid=expect_task.agent_id) to stream step output
Catches a failing step as it happens rather than waiting for full plan completion
Does NOT use the deprecated TaskOutput(block=true) variant
References chain-patterns/references/monitor-patterns.md
